chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: Let me walk you through the solution for the todo list exercise.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: First, we create the HTML with an input field, an add button, and an empty ul element.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: We add some basic CSS to make it look nice with proper spacing and colors.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: In JavaScript, we select the input, button, and ul elements using querySelector.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: We add a click event listener to the add button.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: Inside the event handler, we get the input value and check if it is not empty.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: We create a new li element using document.createElement.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: We set the innerHTML to include the task text, a checkbox, and a delete button.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: We append the li to the ul using appendChild.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: For the delete functionality, we use event delegation on the ul element.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: When a delete button is clicked, we remove the parent li element from the DOM.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: For the checkbox, we toggle a completed class that adds strikethrough styling.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: For localStorage, we create a save function that converts the tasks array to JSON.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: On page load, we check if there are saved tasks in localStorage and render them.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: This is a great beginner project that demonstrates real JavaScript skills.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 Solution - Todo List Complete
chunks.text: In the next video, we will learn about asynchronous JavaScript which is very important.

text: Let me walk you through the solution for the todo list exercise. First, we create the HTML with an input field, an add button, and an empty ul element. We add some basic CSS to make it look nice with proper spacing and colors. In JavaScript, we select the input, button, and ul elements using querySelector. We add a click event listener to the add button. Inside the event handler, we get the input value and check if it is not empty. We create a new li element using document.createElement. We set the innerHTML to include the task text, a checkbox, and a delete button. We append the li to the ul using appendChild. For the delete functionality, we use event delegation on the ul element. When a delete button is clicked, we remove the parent li element from the DOM. For the checkbox, we toggle a completed class that adds strikethrough styling. For localStorage, we create a save function that converts the tasks array to JSON. On page load, we check if there are saved tasks in localStorage and render them. This is a great beginner project that demonstrates real JavaScript skills. In the next video, we will learn about asynchronous JavaScript which is very important.
